title: VLA-OPD: Bridging Offline SFT and Online RL for Vision-Language-Action Models via On-Policy Distillation

headings.text: Online RL with Sparse Outcome Rewards
Mitigating Catastrophic Forgetting via On-Policy Alignment

section_anchors.text: To bridge this gap, we introduce On-Policy VLA Distillation (VLA-OPD), a unified framework that synthesizes the efficiency of SFT with the robustness of RL
In this section, we formalize the VLA training problem and briefly review the two dominant paradigms
We formulate the robotic manipulation task as a Markov Decision Process
Standard VLA training typically starts with SFT on a static dataset of expert demonstrations
However, despite its provenance from LLMs and architectural efficiency, applying GRPO to robotics introduces a unique challenge: feedback sparsity
section_anchors.expected_section: Online RL with Sparse Outcome Rewards

section_anchors.text: VLA-OPD reformulates the alignment process as dense supervision on self-generated trajectories
the training cycle consists of three phases
VLA-OPD discards the static offline dataset after initialization and switches to dynamic on-policy sampling
Instead of relying on sparse outcome rewards that suffer from severe credit assignment issues
Our experimental design addresses several fundamental research questions regarding the efficiency, efficacy, and stability of On-Policy Distillation
Benchmarks and Protocols. We evaluate across two distinct domains to test data-scarce generalization and complex coordination
We compare our method against the baseline GRPO
We analyze catastrophic forgetting via a seen–unseen trade-off
section_anchors.expected_section: Mitigating Catastrophic Forgetting via On-Policy Alignment

section_anchors.text: To validate the architectural design of VLA-OPD, we conduct ablation studies
Recent advancements in robotic manipulation have been largely driven by VLA models
To address the distribution shift inherent in offline SFT, Online RL has been introduced to align models with policy-induced state distributions
In this paper, we propose On-Policy VLA Distillation (VLA-OPD), a novel framework bridging the sample efficiency of offline SFT with the closed-loop robustness of online RL
